park_name: Phillip Island Park Lane Holiday Park

reviews.review_text: This was a great caravan park to stay at, we stayed at an ensuite caravan site, the ensuite was updated and in great clean condition, the location was so good, we were walking distance to a restaurant and next door to Amaze n things, and we were given a 50% off voucher to amaze n things because we stayed at the caravan park. We were able to bring our own fire pit which we could set up close to the kids play areas so the adults could relax while the kids played, highly recommend, we would absolutely stay there again!

We booked a camping pod, basically a luxe cabin, beautiful and modern, stunning shower, best we've had on our trip. Everything we needed including electric blanket on the bed. Staff at reception were extremely friendly and helpful. Little out of the way but Brewery next door for food and drinks if that's your vibe!
Only suggestion would be to change the mat at front door to a rubber mat so the water can drain and u can wipe your feet as it's currently water logged and causing you to take wet/dirty shoes inside despite it not raining. Enjoyed our stay thank you!
Ok but outdated holiday park good if you want to go to amaze n things.

Awesome place 5 min to town
Ensuite big , modern.
Great activities for kids
Restaurant and brewery.
You'll be happy here.
Amazing staff we had an ensuite site was the best we have stayed in new concrete new ensuites. Plenty of play areas for the kids loved mini golf.
ペンギンパレードからの場所も近いし、雰囲気もとても良かったです。ただめちゃくちゃ寒かった。

The room was so tiny ..it was not what we thought we had paid for ..The bed was supposed to be queen but I'm sure it was a double.. not big at all .. The bathroom was clean but very small...There was no table and chairs.. no where to sit ..
....for $228 a night..it was definitely the most disappointing place we have ever stayed.... we ended up packing up and leaving at 10pm and drove home 🏡

Schreckliches Motel! Es sieht nicht so schrecklich aus aber die Fenster sind alt und können nicht richtig geschlossen werden. Alles etwas veraltet, Türe undicht, kalt. Es hat die ganze Nacht durchgezogen. Wir waren im Motel 104. Es ist auch sehr laut. Ausserdem kam ein Mitarbeiter um 9.10 Uhr mit dem Schlüssel in unser Zimmer und weckte meine Freundin. Also das ist echt eine Frechheit. Sie haben nicht einmal im Griff wer schon ausgecheckt hat.
Staying at the park we had a incident that was unrelated to the park and the park staff including the manager were extremely supportive and helpful.

Our initial powered site wasn't great. In an older area which looked less maintained.
So we Moved to an ensuite site. Wow. It was one of the nicest we have seen.  All the staff were helpful & friendly.
Great spot to travel to Race Track from.

We had a fantastic stay at Phillip Island Park Lane Holiday Park! (13-15 Feb 2026)

The amenities were excellent – the toilets were always very clean, and the common kitchen area was spotless and well maintained. The staff were friendly, welcoming, and very helpful throughout our stay.

The swimming pool was nice and clean, and the kids absolutely loved it. The powered sites were spacious and convenient. The only small downside was that the grass-free areas became a bit muddy and dusty at times, but overall it didn’t take away from our great experience.

Highly recommend this park for families and anyone looking for a comfortable and well-kept place to stay on Phillip Island!
Review TBA. Worst experience ever…..

Cabin for 6- walls dirty, cutlery drawer dirty, plates & cutlery only for 4, very bare kitchen equipment, gardens, parking areas, around the pool- overgrown/unkept the only 2 sun lounges removed at the pool  whilst we were  there as visitor complained how dangerous they were with broken legs, laundry filthy and half the machines not working.
Worst Caravan Park l have been to, sorry.

We booked a last-minute powered site for one night at $77, and the experience was extremely disappointing.

While the entrance of the park looks tidy and the online photos are appealing, the reality of our allocated site was very different. We were placed at the very back of the park, directly next to a run-down permanent caravan that made the area feel neglected and unwelcoming.

The site itself was entirely crushed rock, rough, jagged, and completely unsuitable for a caravan site. We couldn’t walk or sit comfortably outside at all. Even worse, we discovered loose screws throughout the crushed rock while reversing in, posing a serious safety risk and potential tyre damage. Behind the site was long, unmaintained grass behind a wire fence, adding further to the run-down feel.

The amenities were equally unacceptable. The women’s bathrooms were filthy, with human hair covering the floors. The men’s bathrooms offered virtually no privacy — my husband (6ft tall) could not shower without being visible from outside the cubicle due to extremely low doors. There appeared to be only 3 small, poorly maintained showers and toilets.

For $77 per night, this was extremely overpriced. I could potentially justify that rate for the better-maintained sites near the front of the park, but absolutely not for the site and facilities we were given.

Due to the condition of the site and amenities, we chose not to stay and drove home late that night instead. We also contacted management directly to raise these concerns and request a refund, but never received a response.

Unfortunately, this stay did not match what is advertised online, and we would not recommend staying here based on our experience.

We stayed here for 3 nights in a ensuite which was near new and modern. Very happy until...we went down to the camp kitchen, games room and pool...very disappointed that I'm not the only family that complained about the gross green pool that has so much filth and what looks like sand(staff at reception told me is sand but is still safe to swim in) on the bottom. They are not doing anything to remove the sand or filth from the bottom of the pool and keep saying its safe to swim let alone children! I would be putting the money we pay to stay there to good use and cleaning the pool until its BLUE not green! False advertising that you have a clean blue pool on your website ans yet we get there and find a green pool with sand and filth on the bottom. Disgusting.
I dont know where this parks money goes to cos the camp kitchen is so old and run down. The fridge and freezer are from the 90s it seems and we had to fight for space for our food and drinks in it!! Then the bbqs we actually had to pay to use! And the games room every game to pay.
I think this park relies heavily on you spending money at amaze and things cos otherwise your kids will get bored. No pool, no free games, no jumping pillows. This is the crappiest big4 partner or not park we have stayed at and we have stayed at a lot!
Would not recommend you stay here with kids and have a hard think during summer cos the pool is dirty and can't use.
Never again. Will def go to the other big4 philip island next time!

The location is good, but the cabin was not clean enough, it had funny smell, so we bought our own air freshener.  Air it out before next guests arrive. The kitchen was dirty, had hardly any cutlery or dishes. The cabin is for 6 people, there were only 5 chairs at the table, room has a small 2-seater couch. Bunk beds - one of the top bunks does not have protective railing, the bottom sheets looked and felt dirty, not fresh and clean.  The front of the cabin has a decking with a table, a bench and some chairs, but everything was too dirty to seat there, not just dust dirty, but covered with cobwebs, not been cleaned for a long time. The swimming pool ... with the green water and slimy bottom .. we were told it was because of some sort of sand that was dumped in it by someone, that it was tested regularly and safe to use, but no ... why not clean it out immediately as there were other complaints about it already.|All in all, more effort needs to be put into cleanliness and maintenance ... but the location is good for visiting nearby attractions. Still, expected more ... after seeing the  photos of the cabin, the swimming pool and the park itself.
Decent campsite for exploring Phillip Island.

Booked a powered site only to find that the site on a slope not easy to level a caravan.
Also staff start 6.30 in the morning to bad if you wanted a quite morning. Besides that it was ok not the best one l have stayed at.

We stayed a couple of nights early Jan and just LOVED it. The park is great! Everything you need is there and you are really close to everything in town. We stayed in a lovely cabin that had a nice bath and lovely front deck which was the perfect spot for breakfast. Hope to return again soon! Would happily recommend to families, couples and solos! Just a lovely stay! ☺️🤩😊
Stayed here april last year. Very clean and amazing cabin. Highly recommend.

VERY AVERAGE. grounds super messy, piles of branches everywhere, leaf drifts. Pool GREEN, not because its painted green either!  Mini golf area unkempt with leaves and scattered tanbark and rolls of fake grass lying around. Clean it up@

The swimming pool was green amid the summer. The game room was left to rot, kids lost 4$ with non maintained games.
Kitchen dirty.
People at the reception lovely.
Too bad.

Really disappointed with this Big4 partner camp. The area where we stayed had rubbish around our site, and the whole place was dusty and poorly maintained — not ideal at all, especially with kids.
There was only one toilet for the entire camping area, and it was very far from our site, which made things extremely inconvenient for families.
No grass anywhere, just dirt, so the kids had nowhere clean or comfortable to play.
The swimming pool was dirty, which was very disappointing and definitely not what you expect from a family-friendly holiday park.
Also, the washing machine, dryer and barbecue all needed coins to operate — nothing like the quality of a real Big4 park.
Overall, we left extremely unhappy. Not family-friendly and far below what you would expect from a Big4 partner park.

Booked to stay at an unpowered site and upon arrival we discovered the sites were directly adjacent to the main road which carries all traffic in and out of Phillip Island. The listing was quite deceptive as there is no mention of this and the photos don’t make this clear.
Stayed in the Safari tent and what a beautiful type of accomadation. Everything was of top quality and provided a very comfortable stay. We will definitely be back.

When we first arrived I was wrapped we opened the door and the rooms were clean and very inviting. It’s also  a short drive to coffee shops, beaches and activities.
However this is where it comes to and end. I spent our time trying to convince myself that this is a good holiday park when it wasn’t. We had a big family booking with lots of kids and it was very frustrating when the pool was green. I didn’t let the kids swim as I was scared the kid could get sick. We spoke to the team at the park about the pool and were told that they think a part might be broken or something? But they didn’t know. We asked if they have checked if it was safe for swimming? They told us they have checked the water with a sample and it’s fine. They didn’t seam like they had a clue on what’s wrong with the pool or that they were trying to get it fixed. The holiday park also needs a lot of maintenance done as there are lots of broken things ie. broken basketball ring, broken soccer nets broken washing machines and broken dryers! Only one working washing machine and dryer working and the washing machine was so tiny I would only be able to wash 2-3 people’s cloths for that day. Not impressed.
We also went to the kids playground and communal kitchen and games room. Not happy the areas were always dirty and messy needs a lot of TLC as they probably haven’t done anything with the communal areas since the 90s.
We also told the team at the park about things that weren’t working in our rooms and nothing was done about them. I also didn’t like how we had to buy dish soap and toilet paper as they don’t keep the rooms stocked through out your stay and some of our rooms didn’t even get supplied with an extra roll of toilet paper. Not a big deal when you travel with a small group however we were travelling with 4 families and grandparents. Very disappointing that when we were on our holiday we had to leave the holiday park to enjoy ourselves. I’m annoyed that my family couldn’t relax around a pool, couldn’t sit out front of our rooms of a night and chat and play as no lights came on and couldn’t turn the outside light on either. The kids couldn’t play with their scooter as it was all gravel and nothing paved and no proper walk way to playground and communal areas.

Our family of four enjoyed a wonderful stay in a standard cabin for 5 nights. The second bedroom had 2 singles in an L shape which was perfect for fitting a portacot and honestly a much more comfortable layout than a room with bunk beds. We were pleasantly surprised with the modern and recently updated kitchen and bathroom facilities. This was also one of the few caravan parks that we could book a year in advance during a peak summer period and at an affordable price point. Proximity to Amaze ‘n’ things was a huge plus, especially with the 50% discount. Our 6yo enjoyed all the activities at Amaze ‘n’ things and the cafe had interesting puzzle and game activities on the tables that made it a much better recreational space for us than the games room at the caravan park (coin operated arcade games). Other than this, there were not many onsite activities for kids and the pool was a suspicious green colour, but not really an issue as beaches and attractions were all within a 5-15 minute drive. One thing to be mindful of is the close proximity to neighbouring cabins can be a bit of a privacy and noise issue but easily fixed by closing the blinds. Thankfully we also had considerate and sensible neighbours. Highly recommend Smith’s beach as well which is only a 5 minute drive, safe for swimming (patrolled by life guards) and beautiful rock pools to explore. Will not hesitate to return to this park again!

The website says that the en-suite sites are suitable for tents.  I was a bit skeptical due to them being slabs so called to confirm and was told that we would be able to pitch our tent on the site.  While this did turn out to be the case, it was only due to very good luck in terms of our tent’s dimensions that we were able to pitch it across the slab, anchoring in the gardens, and it was precarious - if the wind had got up I don’t think the tent would have stayed up.  I wouldn’t recommend the en-suite site for tents at all, I’m not sure why they advise that they’re suitable.  The en suite bathroom itself was obviously very new and was lovely - so I would recommend that if you’re not in a tent!

Camp kitchen was a bit dingy but relatively well equipped and there’s a reasonable basic playground.  We didn’t use the pool - it was very green and cloudy, not inviting at all.  Laundry had several machines out of order - the whole place just felt like it needed some TLC.  We didn’t use the amenities block so can’t comment on those.

We camped in the free camping over the new year period.
This park has the potential to be great but was quite the opposite. The toilets only worked “on occasion”, showers cold and neglected. My partner told me the female bathrooms were afas bad, if not worse. Overflowing sanitary bins! Un flushed toilets due to them not working.
The pool was a horrible cloudy green. I strongly believe that pool would have been a health hazard.
The laundry….. The timer door is so rotted and the glass is about to fall out. 2 washing machines from 1994 (not a fact) and only 1 working.
The camp kitchen is only borderline usable.
I would never stay here again.

Stayed here for 2 nights in our caravan (powered site).
Staff were friendly and helpful upon arrival.
The site itself was average. Gravel site that had some discarded building material scattered. Neighbours had found large screws within the gravel.

The amenities block was terrible. For such a large park that was booked out for new years to have only 3 toilets and 3 showers is minimal. The worst part was the laundry - only 1 washing machine and 1 dryer working, all the others were “out of order”. Perhaps the owners could consider upgrading the facilities.

The swimming pool isn’t blue like the pictures on the website, it is green and gross. Yet people still swimming - a health hazard maybe.

Lastly, one of the nights was New Year’s Eve. We expected some noise but not a park guest with a sound system reverberating throughout the park well after midnight.
For those with kids or like us up early to hit the road found this disrespectful. I would have thought guests would be reminded of reducing noise for the enjoyment of others.
The pool was green. The location was good, shower top was broken. Walls were sticky and wifi was alright
stayed in one of the glamping pods and it was honestly so lovely! booked it the morning of, and had the best mini getaway. perfect location especially if you have kids. we’ll be back for sure!!
Bellissima esperienza in un vero e proprio glamping ! Consigliatissimo

The Phillip Island Park Lane Holiday Park comprises of camp sites, cabins, & motel accommadation.
Reception is manned during business hours.
There is a restaurant on site.
Our room was very basic & compact, but adequate for an overnight stay. The motel was old, & could have used a couple more power points, reading lights & two bedside tables rather than the one. There was no seating available. I prefer not using the bed for sitting.
However, it was very clean & the bed was comfortable.
There were sufficient appliances to make a beverage, toast, or heat a meal (microwave). However, no place to sit & eat, other than the wrought iron setting outside the front door. (It's still too cool for that in the evening).
Although our room would have presented mobility issues, (shower accessability), the complex is large enough to have had suitable accommadation upon request.
We would not hesitate to return.
Home away from home

The staff are very kind and friendly. The price is good for what you get. We stayed on a powered site. It was early spring, so a bit cold at night, but the place was quiet and peaceful. The location is great, and we enjoyed 50% off mini golf. The facilities are okay, not fancy, but fine for the price. The campground is a little old, but good enough for camping.
Excellent

Good location central to everything.   Really lovely friendly staff.   We stayed here as we could take our dog which was wonderful.   We stayed in a standard cabin which is quite small but we were comfortable and warm.  Bed and couch comfortable and shower was good.   Was pretty quiet considering it’s near the main road.   Not good internet connection.
Communication difficile mais bel endroit quand même
Great bed. Had the best sleep

Honestly, the best staff and services provided. Made us feel like we went to a grandparents' house to get spoilt 😂 Thank you Alex and everyone for making our stay so super friendly, thank you for your generosity and care.
Location is supreme, too.

Wonderful last-minute getaway – and dog friendly!

We had a fantastic overnight stay in the Glamping Pods and couldn’t be happier. The pod was spotless, beautifully decorated, and surprisingly spacious – even with our medium-sized dog. We brought our own dog bed and there was plenty of room to sleep comfortably.

One of the biggest highlights for us was being able to bring our dog. It’s such a relief to find pet-friendly accommodation in Victoria, especially when so many places in Australia are still oddly restrictive compared to Europe, where dogs are welcome almost everywhere.

The location was perfect too – we explored a few nearby dog-friendly beaches and had a great time. Not the best photo, but I’ve included one for reference. We’ll definitely be back!
Nice and quiet place. Amenities work.

Very nice well organised place
We appreciated the possibility to bring our dog .
Cabins are extremely clean and well equipped
We highly recommend the place to everyone with dogs, kids and friends
We are sure we will come again.

Stayed in the safari tent for one night, cannot recommend highly enough. Beautiful naked wood inside,  heating and cooling works great in the canvas. The deck is a great touch with a built in undercover BBQ and couch (public BBQs cost $ here). The decor is earthy and classy, staying in the safari tent is an experience.
Staff are absolutely beautiful,  can't fault the service at all.
The park itself is very small, amenities quite old but functional. If you find yourself in a cabin, it's like stepping back into the 90's (don't hate that).
Brewery and restaurant on site, and Amazenthings. Koala conservation tree walk across the road.
WiFi is brilliant.

Just stayed in the Safari tent with our two boys plus Grandpa. The facilities, activities and the whole place is fantastic. Will definitely be checking out their other locations and booking with Park Lane from now on. Make sure you have a round of mini golf and if the kids are brave enough to check out the sky trail!!

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Wonderful stay! Clean, comfy, and well-equipped. Great location and friendly staffs. Would definitely come back and highly recommend!
We stayed in one of the Glamping tents and loved it. It was clean and tidy. Heating worked perfect. Bathrooms clean and easy to use. Staff amazing and helpful. Loved it.

We stayed on an unpowered site for two nights. We booked this caravan park as they advertised as being allowed fire pits. When we arrived we were told there was a total fire ban on the island but when we googled the fire rating on the island it said low rating,  clearly it was just the caravan park rules.

The camp kitchen was filthy  and barely usable – calling it a “kitchen” is a serious stretch.
As for the toilets and showers - seriously outdated and no where to put your clothes.

They also provided Porta potties. Yes, you read that right.

We had to pay to use the bbq facilities. What a joke. For the price we paid, it’s a total insult.

We loved our stay here. We had powdered en-suite sites and couldn’t fault it. The facilities were clean and well-maintained. Staff were friendly and had great communication, we were texted a couple of times while there with just general updates which we haven’t received at any other campsite before. Would definitely come back here in the future.
The bunk bed sheets were not clean and lots of hair and crumbs in the bed.The front office staff were good.

Positive is that it's located centrally in Phillip Island. However the campsite was crammed with people in the unpowered and unreserved area, right next to the main road into Cowes. Loud, noisy, and with a gravel ground (hard for tents), if I had known, would have paid for a room elsewhere opposed to paying for what we got here. Wasn't integrated into nature which is what camping should be, listing to birds not trucks.

Staff were friendly & helpful. Stayed in a motel type room which could do with an upgrade & bit more attention to cleaning. Then stayed in a Safari Tent, this was awesome, couldn't fault. Overall happy with our stay, but we booked there as there was a pool, it was a very hot weekend.. The pool wasn't able to used & we were not told this on arrival, as I'm guessing most weren't, lots of upset kids not able to go for their swim & not finding this out until we were at the pool, dressed in togs, ready to go. This was disappointing.

Although the facilities are a bit outdated, the service is exceptional, and the cleanliness is impeccable, providing a comfortable and good stay. For students, the price is quite reasonable, making it a great value for money. Overall, this motel is definitely worth recommending!

We spent a night on Phillip Island as part of our RV tour of Australia. I saw three holiday parks, looked at their websites and locations, and chose Phillip Island Park for our stay based on reviews and central location on the island. I don't see how I could have made a better choice! Making a reservation was quite easy, and when we arrived, the woman in Reception was very friendly and helpful. I got clear directions and a map, and finding our site was easy. The site itself was very well laid out, clean, with a nice paved pad and well-organized utilities. In addition, to my surprise, we had been upgraded to an ensuite site! The ensuite was very modern, well-equipped, and sparkling clean. We could not have been happier with our stay!
Love this island. Amenities were quite dirty. Awesome camp kitchen although a bit dirty

The caravan site was okay, staff good and the location on the island was great. The only negative is that the shower tiles are very mouldy and knowing that others have made the same comment previously makes me think it’s not a priority for this park. I was gagging when I was in the shower with the sight.
Cosy clean place to stay

Stayed recently for a few days visiting an event. The rooms were tidy and quiet. The area doesn’t get a lot of service and our family cabin at the back of the property didn’t reach the wifi well. We found the location suitable, Cowes only a short drive away. On a main road but we couldn’t hear any traffic. Very well maintained ☺️

Recommend it for short stays if you want to camp. Camped on the unpowered site. It's close to the street and next to the playground so it's little bit noise. But service is pretty good and the staff is helping wherever you need it.☺️

Stayed for 1 night, room was ok, but needs some touch ups and carpet cleaning. There is no shelves or hooks in the room or bathroom, no where you can put you clothes or bath robe. Outside barbecue is not free, which is a bit of a disappointment,  as the price is not cheap. Outdoor facilities are also very old and needs to be replaced/renovated. One highlight is a location.
Pool was filthy was looking forward to a dip in the heat. It had been like that for days. Should be a priority to fix and clean it. Great location

We stayed in our caravan for.3 nights.  Sites were a good size,  however very uneven making it extremely hard to level the van.  Ended up using blocks of concrete to hold it up.   Suggestion to the owners would be to level them out,  as it’s not a good first impression.   There is a camp next door that plays doof doof music until 10pm at night and first thing in the morning.  Does not make for a relaxing camping trip,  might be a good idea to warn guests who are booking about it.  There is no hose by the dump point to wash out your toilet canister,  we have never had a caravan park that does not supply this,  it’s a requirement for hygiene.  The bathrooms were without hot water,  lucky we had our own in our caravan,  but I feel sorry for the other guests that could not shower or wash their hands with warm water.

Location was good,  convenient and we enjoyed the 50% off mini golf.

The park in general needs a bit of investment as its very tired and not really suitable for the number of camp sites.

Very good place to stay. The Koala Conservation Center is just opposite the park. My family and I had a good time there. We cooked by ourselves during our stay which was out of my expectations. My son went to the pool which is suitable for children. The facilities are good. Just the WiFi signal is not good enough which is bit inconvenient for the foreigners like us, because the mobile signal was not that good there as well. But in general, we loved the place and will select park again when the next visit.

A great location . Room was excellent and spacious. We stayed in the family room with 2 bunk beds and a double bed . Bathroom was clean . Love that they have a restaurant within the property and super kids friends as it’s for kids activities place walking distance . Pool was alright size just felt a lot of chlorine was added to the water . Overall a highly budget great place And staff was very friendly with a fast checking in .

We got a cabin for 4 but when we First got in there were loads of ants In the corners on the walls and climbing everywhere. We expected There to be a oven but all there was was a old outdated oven that did not even work so we went down to the camp kitchen but we couldn't use it because there were no Oven Gloves So we couldn't use any oven. Also there was no hot water for 1 day so we couldn't have showers and had to stink for A day and on the basketball court all of the basketball nets were broken. And the worst part You Have to PAY for using the Barbecue With Money and it only last for like 7 minutes! The only good part was the pool but that wasn't perfect either Would not Recommend 😞

We camped for two nights in a tent.
The facilities themselves were ok, camp kitchen was well stocked, games room & pool were clean enough and playground was ok. Staff were friendly. Nice that they ran a daily kids activity, although we weren’t told this at checkin and found this out from family campers.
Some improvements needed.
Found the grounds pretty disappointing in terms of camping. We were initially given a gravel site even though we had booked for a tent site, the second site offered when we complained was between two permanent residents cabins and was quite an ugly outlook. The third site was a little better but still disappointing with just dirt sites and not any bush surrounding it.
Ladies toilet facilities didn’t lock but the men’s did so found this an issue when you wanted to go to the toilet late at night. Workers were inconsiderate, at 7am two men attending to linen in cabins, talked extremely loud with no consideration that people may still be sleeping.
Was also hard to find camp site with very poor site numbers.
Has the potential to be a great park with a good location to Amaze n things and the Koala park but the grounds need some loving to make it more appealing to campers.

We stayed there for 2 nights. We had a family cabin which was spacious & clean. The park is great for kids as it has A Maze n Thing and other facilities such as basketball & Tennis court.
And it is cross the roads from Koalas park.
The swimming pool is not very big but still you can enjoy a lot.
I have already sent the location for two of my friends.

We stayed in the safari tent. It exceeded our expectations as it was so clean and comfortable, even though the weather was a little cool. Staff were great, and helpful. It’s a great location opposite the koala sanctuary. There is also a reserve next door full of wildlife. Excellent spot.
Los unpowered site caros para lo que son

Very expensive for what you get.. would recommend having a look around and booking somewhere else for $250ish per night, Cabins are tiny & beds terribly uncomfortable (this price didn't include any kitchens etc)
Tent/caravan area very badly organised, no signs etc. 3 toilets only (would be a disaster in peak time) and complimentary wifi which sounds great but never worked/constantly cut out.
Sehr gut gelegen, freundliches Personal, Zimmer eher einfach.
Perfect 🙂 🥰

Slowly getting upgraded, toilet and amenities average, has a pool and great for kids with amusement parks close by and brewery.
Staff were lovely.
Great holiday park, suitable for families with kids. Clean rooms, well equipped game room and playground for kids. Friendly staff.

We stayed in a cabin for 3 nights. Very quiet bush setting park. Lots of activities onsite for kids. Make sure to head next door to check out Amaze n Things and the Koala's across the road.

Only a short drive from Cowes, Churchill Island and beaches.

Decent campsite with average facilities. Staff is lovely.

Note if you are in a campervan, the powered sites are quite weirdly located. Ours was in the front yard of someone’s house, which was super odd. Something to be aware of.

Typical cabin campground not really great for actual camping in a van or tent as the site is next to the Main Street without any noise barrier.
Also on a hill and for some reason wet even without rain.
Toilets/showers seem clean but not enough for the whole park
In general it seems like they didn’t put a lot of love into it

Cannot recommend for camping, don’t know about the cabins

We had a basic room for 1 night (yellow door)it was freshly painted, new carpet , & the most comfortable bed. Would highly recommend!!. It was very economical & we could bring our dog. Thanks for a great stay.
Very beautiful park - wonderful weekend

Good location and staff reception were friendly enough.

However the rooms are extremely run down and just beyond old/outdated.
- There were stains on the blind of the couch window
- There were NO TVs In the 1st bedroom (would be an issue for kids who love watching tv at night)
- There were blood stains on the doona cover
- The Couch Was Small and couldn’t fit 3 of us (would also be an issue for 5 people)
- The Pool Was Closed for Chemical Maintenance and we are quite disappointed about this.
- The bunk beds were too short for my grandmother who can’t get out of bed
- the toaster was buggered as it keeps turning the power switch off
- the shower kept spraying water out of the metal
- The cabin was Very small

A few days ago when  we were here We logged A complaint regarding the pool. We weren’t able to use the pool! Very disappointed! 😡

Only good thing was the tv was working fine as it had all the apps.

Definitely needs a lot of maintenance/an upgrade and some modernising!
Might not come here again!
Fräsch, rent och snyggt men alldeles för trångt på damernas toalett, dusch avdelning + att vattenstrålen i duschen var    För klen.

Un beau camping situé pas très loin de la pointe de l'île, sur une route passante, dont on n'entend pas le bruit depuis le camping.
Les emplacements disposent de toutes les commodités. Les douches et les toilettes sont un peu anciennes, mais elles sont propres et fonctionnelles. L'accueil fut fort sympathique

We have been staying at the Phillip Island Park Lane Holiday Park since 2016 for the MotoGP weekend and we have seen some great updates and additions to the cabins and new glamping pods. Its location is close to shops and various restaurant's.
I would highly recommend Park Lane to anybody.

Thankyou to Emily for accomodating us on such short notice, providing a great service for the price, clean kitchen/bbq area and toilet/shower facilities, and even letting us stay beyond checkout time on Sunday our unpowered site, we were given the freedom to pick a spot on Saturday and found it very peaceful at night, away from the main road, so not noisy with cars or wind flapping away, and great lighting yet not disruptive to sleep at the tent, this place is fantastic, right in the middle of the island and a short drive from many great attractions. Highly recommend it for the unpowered sites and visiting the island overnight on a budget. Thanks again Emily and Park Lane.

I stayed  here during AFL weekend during  Sep 2024 and staff were so friendly and humble and definitely consider next time and recommend this accommodation. Near to everything and nice location and accommodation is well maintained and tidy. Thanks for providing all required during stay:).

It was a long weekend and we wanted to go a spontaneous camping trip in Philip island, and this was the only place that was still available to book and pet friendly.
However, we were a bit disappointed by the fact of the camping site is located right next to a main road, the road was busy so it made a lot of noise.
The camping site did not have a designated number/ area for each tent, which made it a guessing game for people to choose where to set up the tent, esp in long holiday, it could be difficult to even pick up a place if you arrive late.
Another fact was that the price of fire pit and firewood is very expensive. It’s 30 bucks for the fire pit for one night, and another 30 bucks for the firewood. I’ve been to different campsites and big chain holiday parks. And those are around 10-15 bucks. I was shocked and tried to convince myself it might be the holiday rate… first time ever I got the idea to just invest a fire pit and bring my own in the future.
Beyond that, all the facilities are good, toilet, camp kitchen etc… there is a restaurant n bar just on the site, although we did not try. It’s close to the adventurous park, walking distance, so I guess it’s more convenient for families who bring kids to relax after having fun in the playground. Staff are friendly and helpful.
Found the tent camping noisy as it’s close to the road and a flood light lite up the whole area over night.

Stayed at the park for two nights for a short get away with our two young kids.
We really liked the location as it seemed central. We also liked the atmosphere of staying in the bush.
Staff were very lovely.
We had a studio cabin, was the perfect size for a family of 4 with young kids. Room was clean and tidy with everything you need in the kitchen and bathroom.
We will definitely be staying here again for our next trip to Phillip Island, and encouraging family to join us. And due to dogs being allowed in the park and in the rooms we will most likely bring our fur baby with us next time too.

I love coming here, i always stay a few days, great place to relax, staff are friendly and welcoming, nothing is to much trouble, cabins are clean and everything you need.
Thankyou
Great park,  great ensuites, friendly staff.

I stayed here in March 2024 and what a lovely experience after staying in this place. People who work here are so humble , I have to really share one unforgettable incident that left me in total surprise. When I was checking out, without knowingly, I lost my ring (24k gold) and that happened to be my wedding one. I thought I lost it somewhere, didn't even knew I had lost in this place, some angel or good hearted person gave my ring to these guys and i had called the next day checking to see if I had dropped ring somewhere in the room. Got a call and to my total surprise, they said they had my ring and rest is like a history to me (unforgettable). I had  some options which I was working out to get back the ring but they finally shipped the ring to my home. Voila! one day I had it,...so this goes to show how the people who are working here are so wonderful and nice guys. About the place , I'm not lying, whole place is fantastic , nice rooms , nice pool, and they have Amaze 'N' things inside their property itself and not only that, location is just perfect for all the attractions and shops. So I highly recommend this place to all.
All you need for a nights stay, quite and right next to amaz 'n' things

Great friendly service and a great price.

Thankyou for an amazing trip and introducing us to Ensuite sites! My husband came back to tell me, and I can say- life has been changed!

What a way to travel. Our little family loved staying with you guys and definitely would not stay anywhere else in Phillip Island.

Great games room for kids, and so conveniently  located being right next door to the time of your life!

Thanks for making our holiday 5 star!

The Cavanagh's xx
Was a totally amazing place, great staff, clean room, and pet friendly,  I highly recommend,  you won't be disappointed 😀

Stayed here last weekend. I accidentally booked for the wrong date and Lani at the front desk went above and beyond to get it fixed and get us checked in. We stayed in one of the Safari tents and to say it was a great experience would be underselling. It was family style clamping. Our two boys (4 & 6) had the time of their lives. It was clean and well set up. The park was close to the penguins, a short drive from the township of Cowes and the half price entry we got to Amaze-n-things located right next door was perfect.

10 out of 10 stay.
美丽的度假旅游区，聚会的圣地，孩子们的游乐场。
I highly recommend Phillip Island Park lane. The cabins are excellent and you can bring your pet. The service is also fantastic.
Very noisy

Did not get a room had a caravan very busy little spot.
Great spot for  phillip island enough away from the busy Cowes but not to far to go for dinner and enjoy the shops and brillant beachs. The caravan park service was great at reception but the entertainment for kids needs a little up grade and pool was freezing cold so needs solar heating even for a very warm day it was too cold too get in. Because Amazing things is next door to the caravan park you get 50% off entry to Amazing things which is a great money savers as it can be a little expensive for a family of 4 but a great day out for the family and a must do as the kids will love it. The park also requires more toilets for such a busy park there were only 2 but the park did hire some toilets when they got busy. A little updating and this could be a brillant spot and would be ranked up there with the best.
Cabins were nice but were poorly cleaned.

In a caravan so didn't access the wide variety of cabins.
Location was central to all points on the island, however not close to shops or the beach - both about 10 min drive.
Located next to Amaze'n'Things and a great restaurant with brewery.

Booked in here a few weeks back, sadly we had a death in our close circle and needed to cancel this booking for this weekend, amongst it all I cancelled and did right thing within 7 days of booking. They have decided to credit me the amount or charge me a $50 fee to cancel. We won’t be staying here for a long time due to change in circumstances so I cancelled. They couldn’t care less about our situation and have charged us a $50 fee. Some peoples circumstances change or sudden things will arise and they’re lack of understanding or good will is disgusting. Once we are through everything and I have more time I will be in contact with the ombudsman to seek further advice. Won’t ever stay here and won’t be telling anyone else to book. Learn compassion and good will. Massive thumbs down as a business

Cannot comment on rooms as stayed on a caravan site. Location is great for family’s to enjoy. Unfortunately the toilet and shower block are very badly maintained. Can accept the fact that they need updating but not the absence of cleaning. Sign inside says cleaned 3 times a day should maybe say 3 times a week. Made an observation ( not really complaining) to lady at counter about the state of the males toilet. She appeared embarrassed for us and apologised but it was still the same that afternoon. Reading other reviews and answers of “watch this space” is no excuse for not sanitising/cleaning the toilet and showers.
Apart from this winge the camp kitchen is great.

Great park.
Everything you need.

What would have been an amazing stay was tarnished by misinformation.
I booked and paid online for family suite. On check in we realised I had accidentally booked and paid for 2 family suites. Was told not to worry, the same thing actually happened just the last week due to wording on website and I would be refunded in full but as I had booked through wotif I would need to go through them for refund.
Cue daily phone calls and check ins during our stay with both wotif and park reception who both told me they were waiting for the other party to confirm.
It took 3 weeks of follow up calls once home to be told the park had denied the cancellation as I did not meet the cancellation period requirements.  An expensive mistake for a room I didn't use and feel the refund was never going to eventuate even though I was assured it would.
Such a shame as the room, location etc were great but this issue and the way it was handled was not ok!

There is space for getting a caravan in and out, well maintained cabins, and other accommodation choices. There is a theme park on site and the Koala Conservation Reserve is across the road. It's a no hassle kind of place, but be sure to leave by 10.

We enjoyed an overnight stay in a studio cabin at Park Lane as part of a brief visit to Phillip Island. We chose this park based on availability and also because it allows dogs in all cabin types.||On arrival, the check-in experience couldn't have been more friendly and we soon located our bright green cabin on the far side of the park. The cabin was modern, very clean and tidy, with a nice outdoor (covered) deck. It was well furnished and equipped with everything we needed (and would need for a longer stay). The air conditioning had been turned on before we arrived, a nice touch on a 37C afternoon!||The park appeared to be well looked after and felt very relaxing, with nice trees and open areas. It was quiet on a Sunday night and we had a great sleep here as a result. The location is good, being a few minutes from Cowes and even closer to dog-friendly Smiths Beach.||Our stay here was excellent. The cabin was good value, the staff super-friendly and allowing dogs gets a big thumbs up from us!
It was better than we expected. The kitchen bathroom, bedrooms were all nice and tidy. A heater provided in the bathroom which was helpful.

Good location and staff on reception were friendly enough.

However the rooms are extremely run down and just beyond old/dated.
- There were cracks in the bathroom tiles.
- The toilet was either made yellow or was so beyond stained it had turned yellow
- There were cobwebs/spiderwebs in almost every corner of the ceiling, spiders in the corners of the ceilings also
- The  blind on the door had a massive stain along with some of the walls in the room, and one small window didn’t have anything covering it so the sun beamed in first thing (would also be an issue with kids who sleep early)
- The tv didn’t work and was the size of a laptop screen
- The door was locked but somehow swung open, lucky we were awake  (imagine this happening whilst we were asleep, anyone could’ve just walked in)
- Room layout was poor
- Expensive for what we got

Only good thing was the aircon working as it was 37 degrees when we arrived

Definitely needs a lot of maintenance/an upgrade and some modernising!
Overall good all staff at reception Emily & Yvette were good

Camped for 10 days. Good grass powered camp site. A good camp kitchen with microwave, stove and oven, BBQ... even a wood fired oven looked good. Tables in camp kitchen had different activities to keep conversation going. A great choice of DVDs and games, plus a games room. A salt water swimming pool. Right across from koala reserve and next to maze. Toilets old but clean.  Office staff advised that owners purchased 18 months ago and had been very run down...gradually doing up.
Only con was toilets not serviced enough... approx 3 days running no toilet paper in morning. As I think only the single amenity block - to me this was controllable.
Team actioned promptly once notified. I would try out the park again.

We spent 4 nights in a cabin at Park Lane

It’s a great location less than a 10 minute drive to Smiths and YCW besch, about 15 minutes from Cowes and about 18 minutes to the Nobbies and Penguins and just a roll out of bed to Amaze N Things.

The cabin was clean and fresh to the standard of a 5 star hotel and is air conditioned so it can be summer outside and winter inside.

It’s good value and we will be coming back for some weekend stays and expect to be regulars over school holidays.

Way over-priced for what you get.
The place isn't bad, but for the price they're charging it should be much, much better (we stayed at decent hotel in middle of Melbourne for cheaper).
Rooms were clean and tidy, however wifi was not working at all. Heard from another customer who messaged their 'after hours service' (no one at reception after 5) to get some non-urgent assistance with their wifi issue and didn't get any response at all.
Not even a 'sorry, we'll get onto it tomorrow'.
Disappointed.

Powered sites not bad  toilets  horrible not friendly service  no one at check in during the day staff never said hi toilets not cleaned in 24 hours while there
Don’t respond to feed back as this was emailed shortly after stay  will not stay again  only stayed there as needed a group booing for long weekend will now stay away

All in all we had a good stay, the staff were all lovely. Unfortunately, the amenities were a let down. They were unclean and often had no toilet paper / hand towel. There were no mops for the 3 showers which service all the camp sites.
The kids really enjoyed the pool and there was direct access to the bike track into Cowes.

Tent sides are beside the street. So a lot of noise. They even put us closest to the street because we were travelling with an infant. A family friendly holiday park would have tried their best to keep the youngest ones as far away from the street as possible.
Facilities were bellow average.
We didn't had much of a choice due to peak season, wouldn't recommend this holyday park even for one night.

We made an error in the size of our caravan at another site but they couldn't accommodate us being peak season, Park Lane were great, they gave us a powered location for a couple of days and then we had to move to a unpowered site for a couple of days. Even though they were really busy. They really saved our vacation. The staff were super friendly and great to deal with. The facilities were really good as well. Will definitely use this park again.

Well located in a peaceful spot, it's not a bad place to stay. We went for one of the colourful cabins which was nice, reasonably well equipped, definitely pokey though. Shower dripped and screen door kept coming off, but not a big deal. Had tea, coffee, sugar, small kitchenette w fridge, nice to eat on the balcony...byo tablecloth. Also byo doona covers.

The park facilities were ok, pool was nice, has a camp kitchen and games room, but really very bare, no couches and paid bbq. These facilities were pretty run down and dirty, as was communal bathroom. Also few bins around, not much recycling.  Has Kids playground but small.

Overall not bad though, we would stay again.

We stayed in a studio cabin and it is spotless!  All the kitchen appliances and utensils are very clean.  The pillows and the queen size mattress are very comfortable, shower is hot and water pressure is strong.  The receptionists at check in and check out are both very nice too.  Highly recommended!

Stayed here for a night with my son and had a great time.
Park was pumping and a great atmosphere with young people camping, families with kids and some older people too.
Tested out the on-site restaurant and was stoked with the food.
We used to stay somewhere else on the island but they went to a 2 night minimum. We can’t always stay 2 nights so this will be our new go-to!

Five star from us all the way, perfect location for what we wanted to do (A Maz N things and penguin parade). Our cabin was spotless and well maintained. Customer service was 5 star and relaxed and friendly conversations. Unfortunately we didn’t have time to experience everything onsite but the pool was a winner with our kids as well as the tennis court. It is amazingly quiet provided the main road is nearby. We will definitely come back.
This place was so clean! Plenty of room, a kingsize bed and two single bunks.
Stayed at an unpowered site. Camp kitchen needs a good deep clean and fire service needs to be updated. Showers were ok. Site itself was clean and tidy.

I stayed here for a short break over the school holidays with my grand daughter. She wanted to visit Amaze n Things (again), so I decided to stay at Park Lane this time as it’s right next door.The Studio Cabin we stayed in was lovely, clean, tidy and in a good spot. It had everything we needed and the bed was very comfy.. The facilities in the park were perfect for kids, play ground, games room, tennis court, but our favourite was the pool. I was  surprised how quiet the park was, even though it’s close to the main road. Overall, a great family park with plenty to keep everyone entertained. Thank you again to all the staff, who were friendly, helpful, it  really showed they love their work… We’ll be back again soon..👍😎

Great location,close to attraction.
Clean room, nice park.
Always excellent stays. Central to everything on PI. Improvement works constantly upgrading cabins. Top shelf
Excellent service and the cabins are very clean and cosy and the location is close to attractions I would recommend it to family and friends
Awesome place for a quick fix Vacation
